B. Tech. Courses offered by Physics Department
Course 1: Quantum Mechanics & Waves and Oscillations
Blackbody radiation and the concept of photons, Quantum mechanical operators, Schroedinger
equation, Wave function, Statistical interpretation, Superposition Principle, Continuity equation
for probability density, Stationary states, Bound states, Free-particle solution, 1-D infinite
potential well, Expectation values and uncertainty relations, 1-D finite potential well, Quantum
mechanical tunneling, Kronig-Penny model and emergence of bands.
General introduction to periodic motion, Simple harmonic motion (SHM), Equation of motion for
SHM, Superposition of 2-vibrations, beats, Lissajous figures, Superposition of N-vibrations,
Undamped and damped free vibrations, Forced vibrations, resonance, Practical examples of
resonance, Coupled oscillations, Normal modes for 2-coupled oscillators, Normal modes for
N-coupled oscillators, Fourier transforms and normal mode analysis, Wave equation,
Super

text: M.Sc. Physics Curriculum Semester 1 Course: History of Science Course contents: Presentation on historical and scientific overview of a discovery/invention on a topic of student’s choice. Course: Electromagnetic Theory Course contents: Electrostatics and Magnetostatics: Laplace and Poisson equation; Boundary value problems; method of images; the concept of Green’s function and its use in boundary value problems; Ampere’s and Biot-Savart law; the concept of a vector potential Electric Field in Matter: Dielectrics; conductors; dielectric constant; polarization; Gauss’s law in dielectrics; bound charge; susceptibility; permittivity. Magnetic Fields in Matter: diamagnets, paramagnets, and ferromagnets; the effect of magnetic field on atomic orbits; magnetization; magnetic field inside matter; magnetic susceptibility and permeability Conservation laws in electrodynamics: Poynting’s theorem; Newton’s third law in Electrodynamics; conservation of momentum Electromagnetic Waves: The wave equat

# Page Title: Physics | IIT Jammu

### Department Vision

In line with the institute's broad vision, the department vision and mission focuses on

- Directional research towards product realization - ideas, devices, processes
- Envisioning a research based-teaching environment
- Research for impact: Becoming the trendsetter
- Popularizing Physics - outreach, local challenges

# Page Title: Physics

- Abhishek Kumar
- Assistant Professor
- abhishek.kumar@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Abhishek Kumar

I am a condensed matter theorist interested in exploring dynamical and transport properties of systems away or close to quantum critical point of certain types. I joined IIT Jammu as an Assistant Professor in March, 2025. I look forward to doing exciting science in collaboration with enthusiastic students and postdocs.

**Recent news from the group:**

* Our new work on resonant Edelstein and inverse Edelstein effects, charge-to-spin conversion, and spin pumping from chiral spin modes has been published in **Physical Review B (Perspective)** [DOI: https://doi.org/10.1103/5p54-96d7] as a contribution to a special issue dedicated to the memory of late Emmanuel Rashba.

* Our recent work on a tight-binding and density-functional investigation of the Raman tensor in 2D massive Dirac fermion systems has been published in **Physical Review B** [https://link.aps.org/doi/10.1103/

##### Publications

**Selected publications:**

1) **Abhishek Kumar**, D. Sénéchal, and A.-M. S. Tremblay, “Cluster dynamical mean-field study of intra-unit-cell charge nematicity in hole-doped cuprates,” Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, vol. 122, no. 10, e2419534122 (2025).

2) **Abhishek Kumar**, V. I. Yudson, and D. L. Maslov, “Quasiparticle and nonquasiparticle transport in doped quantum paraelectrics,” Phys. Rev. Lett., vol. 126, p. 076601 (2021).

3) A. S. Mishchenko, L. Pollet, N. V. Prokof’ev, **Abhishek Kumar**, D. L. Maslov, and N. Nagaosa, “Polaron mobility in the “beyond quasiparticles” regime,” Phys. Rev. Lett., vol. 123, p. 076601 (2019).

4) **Abhishek Kumar**, P. Chandra, and P. A. Volkov, “Phonon-induced collective modes in spin-orbit coupled polar metals,” (Editor’s suggestion) Phys. Rev. B, vol. 108, p. 075162 (2023).

5) **Abhishek Kumar**, P. Chandra, and P. A. Volkov, “Spin-phonon resonances in nearly polar metals with spin-orbit coupling,” Phys. R

# Page Title: Physics

- Arpita Paul
- Assistant Professor
- arpita.paul@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Arpita Paul

##### Research Interest

My research work focuses on first-principles quantum theory of advanced functional materials. It not only aims at achieving microscopic insights into exotic states of fundamentally interesting materials (frustrated magnets, multiferroics, superconductors, Hund’s metals and topological superconductors), but also designing materials of technological importance (multiferroics, superconductors and transparent conductors).

##### Education Qualification

- Ph.D in Physics, 2017, Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research (JNCASR), Bangalore, India
- M.Sc in Physics, 2012, Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati, Assam, India

##### Academic Interests

**Research Areas**

First-principles quantum theoretical modelling of advanced functional materials

##### Research Experience

- September, 2017 - December, 2020: Postdoctoral research associate, De

### Dr. Venkata Sathish Akella

Head of Department

- hod.physics@iitjammu.ac.in

## Dr. Venkata Sathish Akella

#### Message

Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Physics at the
Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu. We pride ourselves as an academic-oriented
research institution where teaching and research are integral components of our mission.
We eagerly seek enthusiastic young minds to engage in undergraduate and postgraduate
courses, as well as pursue Ph.D. studies. Our students are the cornerstone of our
strength.

Within our department, we house a team of highly experienced
and dedicated faculty members who excel both as educators and researchers in their
respective domains. Supported by a proficient group of staff members, we ensure
efficient operations on both technical and administrative fronts. Our driving force lies
in fostering contemporary and fundamental growth across all aspects of our endeavors.

Equipped with state-of-the-art

# Page Title: Physics | IIT Jammu

## Master in Science

The main objective of the M.Sc. (Physics) program is to prepare a talent pool of manpower by inducting them right after their bachelor degree and provide them quality education with sound fundamentals to contribute effectively in these rapidly changing times, and in addition train the students to conduct research in a specific stream of interdisciplinary physics. At the end of the second semester, a student will opt for a stream and allocation will be made as per the availability of the seats and the Department policy. All the admitted candidates irrespective of their streams will undergo a common curriculum for the first two semesters that will cover the core physics courses which form the basis for the courses in the interdisciplinary areas.

Abhishek Kumar is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: abhishek.kumar@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: PhD (Aug 2013 - Aug 2020): University of Florida, Gainesville, USA

Integrated BS-MS (Aug 2008 - Jun 2013): IISER Kolkata, India. Research areas: Quantum polar phase transition, Transport in correlated electron systems, Fermi-liquid with spin-orbit coupling, Theory of Raman, electron spin resonance (ESR) and electric-dipole spin resonance (EDSR) spectroscopies, Strongly correlated systems: cuprates high temperature superconductors, flat-band systems.

Ajeet Kumar Sharma is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Associate Professor. Email: ajeet.sharma@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - PhD in Physics, 2014, Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur
- M.Sc. in Physics, 2008, Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi. Research areas: Biophysics, Systems Biology and Physical Bioinformatics, Thermodynamics and Statistical Physics, Non-equilibrium statistical mechanics and Biophysics, Computational Biophysics, Non-equilibrium kinetics of biological systems. Supervises PhD students: physics:Inayat Ullah Irshad, physics:Mahima, physics:Rupal Chauhan.

Archana Rajput is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: archana.rajput@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - B.Tech (2005-2009), KNIT Sultanpur Uptu Lucknow, 2009
- M.Tech (2010-2012), Delhi University South Campus, 2012
- Phd (2012-2018), IIT Kanpur, 2018. Research areas: Research Areas, Cloaking/Invisbility, Frequency Selective Surfaces, Transformation Optics, Antennas, Metamaterials, Electromagnetics, Network Analysis And Synthesis.

Arpita Paul is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: arpita.paul@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - Ph.D in Physics, 2017, Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research (JNCASR), Bangalore, India
- M.Sc in Physics, 2012, Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati, Assam, India. Research areas: My research work focuses on first-principles quantum theory of advanced functional materials. It not only aims at achieving microscopic insights into exotic states of fundamentally interesting materials (frustrated magnets, multiferroics, superconductors, Hund’s metals and topological superconductors), but also designing materials of technological importance (multiferroics, superconductors and transparent conductors).

Ashok Bera is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Associate Professor. Email: ashok.bera@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: 2012      Ph. D. in Physics Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science, India

2006      M. Sc. in Physics, Vidyasagar University

2004      B. Sc.  Midnapore College, Vidyasagar University. Research areas: Solar cells, Photo-detectors, Photo-thermal water steaming, Resistive Switching Memory, [Research group Info.](https://ashokiacs.wixsite.com/iitjmu-mdseh/blank-2), Interfacial Solar Steam Generation, Opto-electronic device applications, Resistive Switching Memory Devices. Supervises PhD students: physics:Lokesh Kumar Rathore, physics:Parul Garg, physics:Priya Kaith, physics:Vishal Nagar.
Bacham Eswar Reddy is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Professor.

Biswanath Chakraborty is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: biswanath.chakraborty@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: Personal web page [https://sites.google.com/view/biswanathchakraborty/home](https://sites.google.com/view/biswanathchakraborty/home)

- PhD in Physics, 2013, Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore
- M. Research areas: Device Physics, Optics and Photonics, Spectroscopy, Spectroscopy and optics, Single Photon Emitters in Hexagonal Boron Nitride, Spectroscopy and Device Physics of 2D Materials, Spectroscopy of Devices. Supervises PhD students: physics:Dushali Sharma, physics:Pragya Joshi, physics:Sakal Singla, physics:Vishal Suri.

Parveen Kumar is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: parveen.kumar@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - M.Sc., Kurukshetra University, Kurukshetra, 2011
- Ph.D., Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, 2018. Research areas: Open Quantum Systems, Quantum Measurements, non-Hermitian Physics, Quantum Error Correction.

Rahul R Salunkhe is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Associate Professor. Email: rahul.salunkhe@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: **Ph. D.** in Physics (Thin film Physics) 2009.

**M. Sc.** in Physics (Solid state physics) 2005.. Research areas: Solid State Physics, Material Science, Nanotechnology, Ultracapacitor, Nanoporous Materials, Solid-state batteries, Sodium ion battery and capacitors, Solid-state battery. Supervises PhD students: physics:Anwar Hussain, physics:Kavya Prakash Agrim, physics:Neetu Bansal, physics:Prakash Kumar Pathak.

S. R. K. Chaitanya Indukuri is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: chaitanya.indukuri@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - M.Sc, Banaras Hindu University 2009.
- Ph. D, Indian Institute of Science, 2016.. Research areas: Hyperbolic Metamaterial applications, Single plasmonic nanoparticle spectroscopy, Optical Topological Quantum materials, and nonlinear optics of nanomaterials.

Sanat Kumar Tiwari is a faculty member in the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. Designation: Associate Professor. Email: sanat.tiwari@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - BSc: Ewing Christian College (Allahabad University) 2005
- MSc: Banaras Hindu University. 2007
- PH.D. Institute for Plasma Research (Homi Bhabha National Institute). 2014. Research areas: Thermodynamics and transport in strongly coupled plasmas, Magnetized ultracold plasmas, Dynamical behavior of flowing plasmas, Synchronization of dust acoustic waves in dusty plasmas, Computational plasma physics, Computational Plasma Physics, Experimental Plasma Physics. Supervises PhD students: physics:Ab Rauoof Wani, physics:Farida Batool, physics:Sachin Sharma.

Venkata Sathish Akella is the Head of Department (HoD) of the Department of Physics at IIT Jammu. As HoD, they lead the department's academic and research activities. Designation: Head of Department (HoD). Email: sathish.akella@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - B.Sc. (Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry) 1999-2002, Andhra University
- M.Sc. (Physics) 2002-2004, IIT Madras
- Ph.D. (Physics) 2007-2013, Brandeis University, USA. Research areas: Classical Physics, Statistical Physics, Quantum Physics, Electromagnetic Theory, Computational Physics, Interfacial Phenomena, Active Matter (Experimental and Computational).
